Solution

Q4 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

In a study conducted on a reservoir, the saturation vapour pressure of air was observed to be 32 mm of Hg and the relative humidity was 55%. The wind velocity measured at a height of 1.5 m above the water surface was 20 km/h. The daily evaporation loss

(in thousand m3), if water spread of reservoir is 11.2 km2, using Meyer’s equation is ______ .

(Round off your answer to two decimal places)
Use value of Km = 0.36

NAT
Correct answer: 151.76
Solution

151.76 (150 - 153)

 
Q5 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

If the double mass curve analysis of rainfall data for a station shows a sudden change in slope, the most probable cause(s) is/are:

Correct answer: N/A
Solution

(a, c, d)
The causes of inconsistency of rainfall records/sudden change in slope of double mass curve
are:
(i) Shifting of rainfall station to a new location.
(ii) When ecosystem in the vicinity is changed due to land slide, forest fire, flood and earthquake.
(iii) Error in observation from a certain year.
(iv) Change in the vicinity of the station due to growth of trees, building, fencing, cutting of
forest nearby changing the wind pattern, etc.
(v) Rainguage may be faulty from a certain period.
(vi) Site of instrument may be changed or replaced without record.

NAT
Correct answer: 4.37
Solution

4.37 (4.2 - 4.5)

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)

A 24 hour storm of 13 cm rainfall occurs over a catchment of 2 km2. The infiltration rate decreases exponentially and eventually becomes constant at 0.3 cm/hr. The initial infiltration capacity obtained from Horton’s curve is 1 cm/hr with Horton’s constant, Kh = 5 hr–1. An IS evaporation pan with coefficient 0.8 indicated a loss of 0.8 cm of the rainfall due to evaporation. The runoff from the catchment is _______.

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)

Q12 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Correctly match the shape of catchment (List I) to the shape of hydrograph obtained for that catchment (List II) and select the correct code:

 
Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
• Shape of the catchment influences the time taken by a water particle in reaching the outlet from the farthest point of the catchment and hence it influences the time after which peak discharge is obtained.
• Fan shaped i.e. nearly semicircular shaped catchments give high peak and narrow hydrographs
while elongated catchments give low-peaked and broad hydrographs.

Q13 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

The annual maximum discharge data of a catchment follows Gumbel’s distribution. The mean and standard deviation of the recorded series are 535 m3/s and 162.93 m3/s, respectively. The number of years of record is 32. The flood discharge (in m3/s) corresponding to a return period of 50 years is _____ .
Use the following information:
• Reduced mean = 0.5380
• Reduced standard deviation = 1.1193
(Round off to nearest integer)

NAT
Correct answer: 1025
Solution

1025 (1024 - 1026)

 
Q14 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

In the moving boat method used for streamflow measurement, which of the following parameters must be measured to accurately determine discharge?

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
In the moving boat method, discharge is calculated by integrating the flow across the stream
cross-section while the boat moves from one bank to the other. The essential parameters include:
1. Velocity and direction of flow
2. Water depth
3. Time interval between successive measurements
• In practice, it is the water velocity vector (relative to boat and earth) that is measured, not
boat speed itself.
